findings.title: Privacy Risk: Lack of Granular Consent Mechanism
findings.description: The current consent mechanism does not allow users to opt out of specific data tracking categories, which is a violation of GDPR requirements and may lead to user distrust.
assumptions: Users require more control over their data tracking preferences.

evidence.excerpt: Users require more granular consent mechanisms for data tracking, as the current model does not meet GDPR requirements.

recommendations: Implement a granular consent mechanism that allows users to opt out of specific data tracking categories.

metadata.mitigation: Develop and implement a tiered consent mechanism.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: A full GDPR compliance audit is required before launch, addressing lawful processing, consent mechanisms, and data retention policies, which is a critical blocker for the EU market.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is essential for operating in the EU.

evidence.excerpt: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

recommendations: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address concerns.

metadata.mitigation: Perform a comprehensive GDPR compliance audit.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Tracking
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with user behavior tracking and data sharing with advertisers, including potential re-identification issues.
assumptions: User data must be handled with care to avoid privacy violations.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant privacy risks associated with user behavior tracking and data sharing with advertisers.

recommendations: Implement strict data handling and anonymization protocols.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance data handling practices to mitigate privacy risks.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses
findings.description: The authentication flow may have weaknesses that could be exploited, leading to unauthorized access to user data.
assumptions: Current authentication mechanisms may not be robust enough.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in 3 source(s).

recommendations: Review and strengthen the authentication mechanisms.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a security review of the authentication process.

findings.title: Compliance Risk: Data Retention Policy Violations
findings.description: The current data retention practices may not comply with the 90-day limit set by policy, risking non-compliance with GDPR.
assumptions: Data retention practices need to be strictly monitored.

evidence.excerpt: Establish a data retention policy that complies with GDPR.

recommendations: Implement a strict data retention policy and monitor compliance.

metadata.mitigation: Create and enforce a data retention policy.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 2 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (3 blockers, 2 warnings).
